title: SSO login redirect loop for users with plus-addressed or aliased emails after Okta-to-Entra-ID migration
problem: After migrating SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users experience an infinite redirect loop when logging in. They authenticate successfully with Microsoft but are immediately redirected back to the identity provider upon returning to TaskFlow. The pattern correlates with users who have plus-addressing in their email addresses (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) or whose emails were aliased during the Entra ID setup. A network-tab observation shows that the Set-Cookie header from the auth callback response does not persist on the subsequent request.
root_cause_hypothesis: The session or auth cookie value is derived from or includes the user's email address. The '+' character in plus-addressed emails is likely being improperly encoded or rejected during cookie creation or session lookup, causing the session cookie to either fail to be set (malformed Set-Cookie header) or fail to match on the next request. This would cause TaskFlow to treat the user as unauthenticated on the callback, restarting the OAuth flow. Okta may have normalized or stripped the plus-addressing before returning the email claim, masking this bug, while Entra ID preserves it.
reproduction_steps: Configure TaskFlow v2.3.1 SSO with Microsoft Entra ID
Create or use a user account whose email contains plus-addressing (e.g., user+tag@company.com)
Attempt to log in to TaskFlow via SSO
Observe: after successful Microsoft authentication, the redirect back to TaskFlow triggers another redirect to Microsoft, creating an infinite loop
Compare with a user whose email has no special characters — login succeeds normally
environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1, self-hosted on-premises, Microsoft Entra ID as SSO provider, multiple browsers affected (Chrome, Edge, Firefox)
impact: ~30% of the team cannot log in to TaskFlow at all. These users are completely blocked from accessing the application. Workarounds (if any) are unknown. The issue spans roles — both regular members and admins are affected.
recommended_fix: 1. Inspect the SSO callback handler where the email claim from the OIDC/SAML token is used to create or look up a user session. 2. Check whether the '+' character (or other special characters) in the email is being URL-encoded, stripped, or otherwise mangled before being written into the session cookie or session store. 3. Check the Set-Cookie header construction — ensure the cookie value is properly encoded and the '+' character is not causing a malformed header that the browser silently rejects. 4. Compare the email claim format returned by Entra ID vs. what Okta returned — Entra ID may preserve plus-addressing while Okta normalized it. 5. Fix email handling to properly encode or normalize emails consistently across the auth flow.
proposed_test_case: Write an integration test that performs the SSO callback flow with an email containing plus-addressing (e.g., test+tag@example.com). Assert that: (a) the session cookie is set correctly in the response, (b) the cookie is accepted and matched on the subsequent authenticated request, and (c) the user is not redirected back to the identity provider.
information_gaps: Exact authentication library/middleware in use (developer can determine from codebase)
Server-side logs from a failed login attempt (developer can pull these during investigation)
Whether the issue also affects users with other special characters in emails beyond '+'
